tEn: Predictability and Surprise in Large Generative Models
tZh: 大型生成模型中的可预测性与意外性
sEn: Large generative models exhibit predictable loss scaling but unpredictable specific behaviors, creating policy challenges.
sZh: 大型生成模型在整体损失上可预测，但具体能力、输入和输出却难以预料，这种矛盾给政策制定带来挑战。
absEn: Large-scale pre-training has recently emerged as a technique for creating capable, general purpose, generative models such as GPT-3, Megatron-Turing NLG, Gopher, and many others. In this paper, we highlight a counterintuitive property of such models and discuss the policy implications of this property. Namely, these generative models have an unusual combination of predictable loss on a broad training distribution (as embodied in their "scaling laws"), and unpredictable specific capabilities, inputs, and outputs. We believe that the high-level predictability and appearance of useful capabilities drives rapid development of such models, while the unpredictable qualities make it difficult to anticipate the consequences of model deployment. We go through examples of how this combination can lead to socially harmful behavior with examples from the literature and real world observations, and we also perform two novel experiments to illustrate our point about harms from unpredictability. Furthermore, we analyze how these conflicting properties combine to give model developers various motivations for deploying these models, and challenges that can hinder deployment. We conclude with a list of possible interventions the AI community may take to increase the chance of these models having a beneficial impact. We intend this paper to be useful to policymakers who want to understand and regulate AI systems, technologists who care about the potential policy impact of their work, and academics who want to analyze, critique, and potentially develop large generative models.
absZh: 大规模预训练近期已成为创建通用生成模型（如GPT-3、Megatron-Turing NLG、Gopher等）的技术。本文强调了这类模型的一个反直觉特性及其政策影响：它们在广泛的训练分布上具有可预测的损失（体现在“缩放定律”中），但具体能力、输入和输出却不可预测。我们认为，高层次的可预测性和有用能力的表象推动了模型的快速发展，而不可预测性则使模型部署的后果难以预料。我们通过文献和实际观察中的例子展示了这种组合如何导致社会危害，并进行了两项新实验以说明不可预测性带来的危害。此外，我们分析了这些矛盾特性如何给模型开发者带来部署动机和挑战。最后，我们提出了一系列可能的干预措施，以增加这些模型产生有益影响的机会。本文旨在帮助理解和管理AI系统的政策制定者、关注工作潜在政策影响的技术人员，以及希望分析、批评和开发大型生成模型的学者。

contrib.en: Identifies paradoxical combination of predictable scaling laws and unpredictable specific capabilities in large generative models.
contrib.zh: 识别出大型生成模型中可预测的缩放定律与不可预测的具体能力之间的矛盾组合。

contrib.en: Demonstrates abrupt emergence of harmful capabilities like recidivism prediction with bias at scale via novel experiments.
contrib.zh: 通过新颖实验证明有害能力（如带偏见的再犯预测）在规模扩大时突然涌现。

contrib.en: Analyzes how predictability drives rapid development while unpredictability hinders consequence anticipation.
contrib.zh: 分析可预测性如何推动快速发展，而不可预测性如何阻碍后果预判。

contrib.en: Documents open-ended inputs and outputs leading to unknown capabilities and harmful outputs like toxicity scaling.
contrib.zh: 记录开放输入和输出导致未知能力及有害输出（如有毒性随规模增长）。

contrib.en: Provides policy interventions including national research clouds, red teaming, and governance structures.
contrib.zh: 提出政策干预措施，包括国家研究云、红队测试和治理结构。

contrib.en: Empirically shows increasing industry-academia gap in large model development and proliferation of deployments.
contrib.zh: 实证显示大型模型开发中产业界与学术界差距扩大，以及部署的激增。

limits.en: Focuses on large language models; findings may not generalize to other generative modalities.
limits.zh: 聚焦大型语言模型；结论可能不推广至其他生成模态。

limits.en: Novel experiments limited to recidivism prediction and toxicity; other harms may differ.
limits.zh: 新颖实验限于再犯预测和毒性；其他危害可能不同。

limits.en: Does not address labor, supply chain, or environmental costs of large models.
limits.zh: 未涉及大型模型的劳动力、供应链或环境成本。

limits.en: Policy interventions are speculative and lack empirical validation of effectiveness.
limits.zh: 政策干预措施是推测性的，缺乏有效性的实证验证。

limits.en: Analysis of motivations and barriers is qualitative and may not capture all factors.
limits.zh: 动机和障碍分析是定性的，可能未涵盖所有因素。

items.en: Large-scale pre-training has recently emerged as a technique for creating capable, general-purpose, generative models such as GPT-3, Megatron-Turing NLG, Gopher, and many others. In this paper, we highlight a counterintuitive property of such models and discuss the policy implications of this property. Namely, these generative models have a paradoxical combination of predictable loss on a broad training distribution (as embodied in their "scaling laws"), and unpredictable specific capabilities, inputs, and outputs. We believe that the high-level predictability and appearance of useful capabilities drives rapid development of such models, while the unpredictable qualities make it difficult to anticipate the consequences of model deployment. We go through examples of how this combination can lead to socially harmful behavior with examples from the literature and real world observations, and we also perform two novel experiments to illustrate our point about harms from unpredictability. Furthermore, we analyze how these conflicting properties combine to give model developers various motivations for deploying these models, and challenges that can hinder deployment. We conclude with a list of possible interventions the AI community may take to increase the chance of these models having a beneficial impact. We intend this paper to be useful to policymakers who want to understand and regulate AI systems, technologists who care about the potential policy impact of their work, funders who want to support work addressing these challenges, and academics who want to analyze, critique, and potentially develop large generative models.
items.zh: 大规模预训练最近已成为一种创建能力强、通用、生成式模型的技术，例如GPT-3、Megatron-Turing NLG、Gopher等。在本文中，我们强调了这类模型的一个反直觉特性，并讨论了该特性的政策影响。即，这些生成式模型具有一种矛盾组合：在广泛的训练分布上具有可预测的损失（如其“缩放定律”所体现），同时具有不可预测的具体能力、输入和输出。我们认为，高层次的可预测性和有用能力的出现推动了这类模型的快速发展，而不可预测的特性使得难以预见模型部署的后果。我们通过文献和现实世界观察中的例子，展示了这种组合如何导致对社会有害的行为，并进行了两项新颖的实验来阐明关于不可预测性危害的观点。此外，我们分析了这些相互冲突的特性如何结合，为模型开发者提供了部署这些模型的各种动机，以及可能阻碍部署的挑战。最后，我们列出了一些AI社区可能采取的干预措施，以增加这些模型产生有益影响的机会。我们希望本文对希望理解和监管AI系统的政策制定者、关心其工作潜在政策影响的技术人员、希望支持应对这些挑战工作的资助者，以及希望分析、批评并可能开发大型生成式模型的学者有所帮助。

items.en: Scaling up the amount of data, compute power, and model parameters of neural networks has recently led to the arrival (and real world deployment) of capable generative models such as CLIP (Radford et al., 2021), Ernie 3.03.03.0 Titan (Wang et al., 2021), FLAN (Wei et al., 2021), Gopher (Rae et al., 2021), GPT-3 (Brown et al., 2020), HyperClova (Kim et al., 2021), Jurassic-1-Jumbo (Lieber et al., 2021), Megatron Turing NLG (Smith et al., 2022), LaMDA (Thoppilan et al., 2022), Pan Gu (Zeng et al., 2021), Yuan 1.01.01.0 (Wu et al., 2021), and more. For this class of models111Some refer to this class of models as ‘foundation models’ (Bommasani et al., 2021). the relationship between scale and model performance is often so predictable that it can be described in a lawful relationship — a scaling law. In most cases, these scaling laws predict a continued increase in certain capabilities as models get larger. At the same time, larger generative models represent an increasing proportion of the eye-catching results in machine learning. As a result, many institutions have started producing large models over the past few years, in response to the predictability afforded by scaling laws, and the fact these models can be plugged into systems that generate economic value, like search engines.222We do not discuss to whom this economic value accrues, and we do not intend to imply that by default it will accrue broadly or that no one will be harmed. It has also become clear that these models present novel risks of harmful behavior, which are difficult to predict and may become more severe as the models increase in capability. Attempts to study these harms with smaller models may not accurately reflect what occurs in larger ones.
items.zh: 最近，扩大神经网络的数据量、算力和模型参数导致了强大生成式模型的出现（和现实世界部署），例如CLIP (Radford et al., 2021)、Ernie 3.0 Titan (Wang et al., 2021)、FLAN (Wei et al., 2021)、Gopher (Rae et al., 2021)、GPT-3 (Brown et al., 2020)、HyperClova (Kim et al., 2021)、Jurassic-1-Jumbo (Lieber et al., 2021)、Megatron Turing NLG (Smith et al., 2022)、LaMDA (Thoppilan et al., 2022)、Pan Gu (Zeng et al., 2021)、Yuan 1.0 (Wu et al., 2021)等。对于这类模型，规模与模型性能之间的关系通常非常可预测，以至于可以用一种规律性关系——缩放定律——来描述。在大多数情况下，这些缩放定律预测随着模型变大，某些能力会持续提升。同时，更大的生成式模型在机器学习中引人注目的结果中占据了越来越大的比例。因此，许多机构在过去几年中开始生产大型模型，以响应缩放定律提供的可预测性，以及这些模型可以接入生成经济价值的系统（如搜索引擎）这一事实。我们也清楚这些模型呈现出新的有害行为风险，这些风险难以预测，并且随着模型能力增强可能变得更加严重。用较小模型研究这些危害的尝试可能无法准确反映较大模型中的情况。

items.en: In this paper, we attempt to better understand the influence of scaling laws on the dynamics of large-scale model development and deployment, with a focus on large language models. Our basic thesis is that large generative models have a paradoxical combination of high predictability — model loss improves in relation to resources expended on training, and tends to correlate loosely with improved performance on many tasks — and high unpredictability — specific model capabilities, inputs, and outputs can’t be predicted ahead of time. The former drives rapid development of such models while the latter makes it difficult to anticipate the consequences of their development and deployment. We go through examples of how this combination can lead to socially harmful behavior, while also analyzing the motivations and challenges that developers of such models will face. Our goal in this paper is to outline how and why we expect these models to be developed, so we can identify interventions to guide model development. We conclude with some policy recommendations that could increase the safety of large-scale model deployments, and improve the incentive structure for developers building these models. Though all of the individual points about scaling laws, open-endedness, or the proliferation of large models are explicitly or implicitly presented in other research, our contribution here is to highlight the complete picture together with its implications.
items.zh: 在本文中，我们试图更好地理解缩放定律对大规模模型开发和部署动态的影响，重点关注大型语言模型。我们的基本论点是，大型生成式模型具有一种矛盾组合：高可预测性——模型损失相对于训练投入的资源而改善，并且通常与许多任务上的性能提升松散相关——和高不可预测性——特定的模型能力、输入和输出无法提前预测。前者推动了这类模型的快速发展，而后者使得难以预见其开发和部署的后果。我们通过例子展示这种组合如何导致对社会有害的行为，同时分析这类模型开发者将面临的动机和挑战。本文的目标是概述我们预期这些模型将如何以及为何被开发，以便我们能够识别干预措施来指导模型开发。最后，我们提出一些政策建议，这些建议可以增加大规模模型部署的安全性，并改善构建这些模型的开发者的激励结构。尽管关于缩放定律、开放性、或大型模型扩散的所有个别观点在其他研究中都有明确或隐含的呈现，但我们的贡献在于强调完整的图景及其影响。

items.en: Although we focus on scaling laws, many of our points complement related work on the societal risks of deploying large models (Bender et al., 2021; Tamkin et al., 2021; Bommasani et al., 2021; Dinan et al., 2021; Weidinger et al., 2021; Kenton et al., 2021). However, similarly to (Weidinger et al., 2021), we do not consider here the costs of human labor involved in creating and annotating training data (Gray and Suri, 2019), the ethics of supply chains involved in creating the requisite hardware on which to train models (Crawford, 2021), or the environmental costs of training models (Bender et al., 2021; Patterson et al., 2021; Schwartz et al., 2020; Strubell et al., 2019). Scaling laws are likely to significantly impact these issues.
items.zh: 尽管我们专注于缩放定律，但我们的许多观点与关于部署大型模型的社会风险的现有工作相辅相成 (Bender et al., 2021; Tamkin et al., 2021; Bommasani et al., 2021; Dinan et al., 2021; Weidinger et al., 2021; Kenton et al., 2021)。然而，与 (Weidinger et al., 2021) 类似，我们在此不考虑创建和注释训练数据所需的人力成本 (Gray and Suri, 2019)、创建训练模型所需硬件的供应链伦理 (Crawford, 2021)、或训练模型的环境成本 (Bender et al., 2021; Patterson et al., 2021; Schwartz et al., 2020; Strubell et al., 2019)。缩放定律很可能对这些议题产生重大影响。

items.en: The remainder of the paper is organized as follows. In Section 2, we articulate and support our central thesis about large generative models by decomposing it into four claims, each of which we support with evidence from previously published data, and in some cases, with novel experiments on large language models (Askell et al., 2021). In Section 2.1 we discuss smooth general capability scaling. More precisely, by general capability scaling we mean two things. First, the training (and test) loss improves predictably with scale on a broad data distribution. Second, this improvement in loss tends to correlate on average with increased performance on a number of downstream tasks (Brown et al., 2020; Rae et al., 2021). We refer to the combination of these two properties throughout the paper as smooth general capability (or performance) scaling.333Note that, as will be discussed later as the central thesis of the paper, smooth general capability scaling does not imply smooth scaling on any particular task. It also does not imply that the tasks typically measured are the only tasks that are important; indeed the presence of unmeasured tasks is part of our thesis. In Section 2.2, we discuss abrupt specific capability scaling, in which models can also suddenly gain specific capabilities at scale. We illustrate this phenomenon with three examples from the literature (Brown et al., 2020; Rae et al., 2021; Austin et al., 2021). In Section 2.3, we argue that entire areas of model competency may be unknown until they are solicited from specific inputs, problem domains, or applications. In Section 2.4, we discuss challenges that arise from the open-endedness of model outputs and show both qualitative and quantitative examples of harmful and toxic outputs emerging with scale.
items.zh: 本文的其余部分组织如下。在第2节中，我们阐述并支持我们关于大型生成式模型的核心论点，将其分解为四个主张，每个主张我们都用先前发表的数据证据支持，在某些情况下，还通过大型语言模型的新颖实验 (Askell et al., 2021) 支持。在第2.1节中，我们讨论平滑的一般能力缩放。更准确地说，一般能力缩放指两件事。首先，训练（和测试）损失随着规模在广泛数据分布上可预测地改善。其次，这种损失的改善平均上与许多下游任务性能的提升相关 (Brown et al., 2020; Rae et al., 2021)。我们在整篇论文中将这两个属性的组合称为平滑的一般能力（或性能）缩放。注意，正如后面将作为本文核心论点讨论的，平滑的一般能力缩放并不意味着任何特定任务上的平滑缩放。它也不意味着通常测量的任务是唯一重要的任务；事实上，未测量任务的存在是我们论点的一部分。在第2.2节中，我们讨论突发的特定能力缩放，其中模型也可能在规模扩大时突然获得特定能力。我们用文献中的三个例子说明这一现象 (Brown et al., 2020; Rae et al., 2021; Austin et al., 2021)。在第2.3节中，我们认为模型能力的整个领域可能直到从特定输入、问题领域或应用中引出时才被知晓。在第2.4节中，我们讨论模型输出开放性带来的挑战，并展示随着规模扩大出现有害和有毒输出的定性和定量例子。

items.capEn: Figure 1. Scaling laws reliably predict that model performance (y-axes) improves with increasing compute (Left), training data (Middle), and model size (Right). In all cases a power-law (straight line, black) fits the empirically observed data (blue) exceptionally well. Figure adapted from (Kaplan et al., 2020).
items.capZh: 图1. 缩放定律可靠地预测模型性能（y轴）随着算力（左）、训练数据（中）和模型大小（右）的增加而提升。在所有情况下，幂律（直线，黑色）与经验观察数据（蓝色）拟合得非常好。图改编自 (Kaplan et al., 2020)。

items.en: In Section 3, we outline why, despite these conflicting properties of predictability and unpredictability, we expect increasing development and deployment of large generative models despite the challenges we outline in Section 2. We posit that this is due to a confluence of economic, scientific, and prestige motivations, each of which we summarize. We also consider a few possible barriers to entry that model developers may face during development and deployment, including high financial costs, access to engineering talent, safety concerns, and a lack of standards on how to responsibly deploy capable generative models. We also provide some empirical observations (grounded in the motivations and challenges described above) about how the development of large language models has unfolded thus far, including a quantitative analysis of the increasing gap between academia and industry for large model development.
items.zh: 在第3节中，我们概述为什么尽管存在可预测性和不可预测性这些相互冲突的特性，我们仍预期大型生成式模型的开发和部署会增加，尽管我们在第2节中概述了挑战。我们假设这是由于经济、科学和声望动机的汇合，我们对每个动机进行了总结。我们还考虑了模型开发者在开发和部署过程中可能面临的一些进入障碍，包括高财务成本、工程人才获取、安全问题，以及缺乏如何负责任地部署强大生成式模型的标准。我们还提供了一些关于大型语言模型开发迄今为止如何展开的实证观察（基于上述动机和挑战），包括对学术界和工业界在大型模型开发方面日益扩大的差距的定量分析。

items.en: Finally, in Section 4 we outline policy interventions that may help concretely address the challenges we outline in Sections 2 and 3 in order to help guide the development and deployment of larger models for the broader social good. We leave some illustrative experiments, technical details, and caveats about our claims in Appendix A.
items.zh: 最后，在第4节中，我们概述了可能有助于具体解决我们在第2节和第3节中概述的挑战的政策干预措施，以帮助引导更大模型的开发和部署，造福更广泛的社会。我们将一些说明性实验、技术细节和关于我们主张的注意事项留在附录A中。

full.sec: 2. Distinguishing Features of Large Generative Models
full.secZh: 2. 大型生成模型的显著特征

items.en: We claim that large generative models (e.g., GPT-3 (Brown et al., 2020), LaMDA (Thoppilan et al., 2022), Gopher (Rae et al., 2021), etc.) are distinguished by four features:
items.zh: 我们声称大型生成式模型（例如，GPT-3 (Brown et al., 2020)、LaMDA (Thoppilan et al., 2022)、Gopher (Rae et al., 2021) 等）具有四个显著特征：

items.en: Smooth, general capability scaling: It is possible to predictably improve the general performance of generative models — their loss on capturing a specific, though very broad, data distribution — by scaling up the size of the models, the compute used to train them, and the amount of data they’re trained on in the correct proportions. These proportions can be accurately predicted by scaling laws (Figure 1). We believe that these scaling laws de-risk investments in building larger and generally more capable models despite the high resource costs and the difficulty of predicting precisely how well a model will perform on a specific task. Note, the harmful properties of models, such as toxicity, can scale alongside directly helpful capabilities.
items.zh: 平滑的一般能力缩放：通过按正确比例扩大模型大小、用于训练的算力和训练数据量，可以可预测地改善生成式模型的一般性能——它们在捕获特定但非常广泛的数据分布上的损失。这些比例可以通过缩放定律准确预测（图1）。我们认为，这些缩放定律降低了构建更大且通常能力更强的模型的投资风险，尽管资源成本高且难以准确预测模型在特定任务上的表现。注意，模型的有害属性（如毒性）可能与直接有益的能力一起缩放。

items.en: Abrupt, specific capability scaling: Though performance is predictable at a general level, performance on a specific task can sometimes emerge quite unpredictably and abruptly at scale.444Similar behavior has also been observed during the training process of an individual model (rather than as a function of model size) for algorithmic tasks, and has been termed “grokking” (Power et al., 2022). While counter-intuitive, this is possible because any specific task is a tiny slice of a model’s output probability distribution, and so can change rapidly even as the full distribution remains smooth.
items.zh: 突发的特定能力缩放：尽管在一般水平上性能是可预测的，但在特定任务上的性能有时会非常不可预测且突然地在规模扩大时出现。虽然反直觉，但这是可能的，因为任何特定任务都是模型输出概率分布的一小部分，因此即使整个分布保持平滑，它也可能快速变化。

items.en: Open-ended inputs and domains: Large generative models are open-ended and can take in a varying range of inputs concerning arbitrary domains. As a result, certain capabilities (or even entire areas of competency) may be unknown until an input happens to be provided that solicits such knowledge. Even after a model is trained, creators and users may not be aware of most of its (possibly harmful) capabilities. These properties become more pronounced as the models scale — larger models tend to be harder to characterize than smaller ones.
items.zh: 开放的输入和领域：大型生成式模型是开放的，可以接受涉及任意领域的各种输入。因此，某些能力（甚至整个能力领域）可能直到恰好提供了引出此类知识的输入时才被知晓。即使在模型训练完成后，创建者和用户也可能不知道其大部分（可能有害的）能力。随着模型规模扩大，这些特性变得更加明显——较大的模型往往比较小的模型更难表征。

items.en: Open-ended outputs: Finally, model outputs are also open-ended in the sense that they are difficult to predict or control, even given a fixed scale, input, topic, or task. These outputs may be helpful or harmful, but it’s difficult to know in advance. Of course, models with both open-ended inputs and outputs have existed for decades, but what is new is the level of capability and breadth of open-endedness.
items.zh: 开放的输出：最后，模型输出也是开放的，因为即使给定固定的规模、输入、主题或任务，它们也难以预测或控制。这些输出可能有益或有害，但很难提前知道。当然，具有开放输入和输出的模型已经存在了几十年，但新的是能力水平和开放性的广度。

items.en: In the following sections, we further describe each of these distinguishing features, and discuss how combinations of them may lead to disruptive societal impacts. We support our claims with data and experiments.
items.zh: 在以下小节中，我们进一步描述这些显著特征中的每一个，并讨论它们的组合如何可能导致破坏性的社会影响。我们用数据和实验支持我们的主张。

items.en: Generally, machine learning experiments are not precisely predictable — complex models trained on complex data typically yield noisy or variable results (Zhuang et al., 2021; Clary et al., 2019).555For example, (Clary et al., 2019) documents a strong lack of run-to-run reproducibility in reinforcement learning on Atari games when only changing the initial random seed. This suggests that differences between algorithms may be difficult to measure rigorously due to such intrinsic noise. Though individual experiments may be unpredictable, the general performance of large generative models tends to exhibit smooth and predictable growth as a function of scale — larger systems tend to do increasingly better on a broad range of tasks. This was first noticed by (Hestness et al., 2017) who observed that capabilities such as machine translation and speech recognition increased in a smooth, predictable manner as the size of the model increased. Subsequent work formalized and experimentally validated a quantitative relationship between scale (in terms of both model size and training data size) and model generalization error (Rosenfeld et al., 2019). Furthermore, (Kaplan et al., 2020) demonstrated that test loss performance on language modeling tasks scales as a predictable function of model size, dataset size, and duration of training. These three factors are like ingredients in a chemical reaction, such that if all are scaled up in tandem, the test loss improves proportionally. However, if there is too little of one ingredient, gains are limited by this ingredient. The trends are remarkably consistent, with only tiny deviations from a simple fit to the data666More precisely, the relationship is a straight line on a log-log plot, equivalent to a power law., covering dozens of data points and several orders of magnitude (Figure 1).777Scaling naturally has a fundamental limit: the entropy of the training and test data sets. However, it’s both difficult to precisely estimate this quantity a-priori and perhaps likely that there are important model capabilities that may emerge while pursuing this limit asymptotically. Subsequent work has shown that similar scaling laws exist in generative models for other modalities (e.g., images, video, math, etc.) (Henighan et al., 2020), audition (Droppo and Elibol, 2021), transfer from text to programming (Hernandez et al., 2021), few-shot adaptation of vision models (Prato et al., 2021), and more.
items.zh: 通常，机器学习实验并非精确可预测——在复杂数据上训练的复杂模型通常会产生噪声或可变的结果 (Zhuang et al., 2021; Clary et al., 2019)。例如，(Clary et al., 2019) 记录了在Atari游戏上强化学习在仅改变初始随机种子时缺乏运行间可重复性。这表明由于这种固有噪声，算法之间的差异可能难以严格测量。尽管个别实验可能不可预测，但大型生成式模型的一般性能往往表现出随规模平滑且可预测的增长——较大的系统通常在广泛任务上表现越来越好。这最初由 (Hestness et al., 2017) 注意到，他们观察到机器翻译和语音识别等能力随着模型大小增加而以平滑、可预测的方式提升。后续工作形式化并实验验证了规模（在模型大小和训练数据大小方面）与模型泛化误差之间的定量关系 (Rosenfeld et al., 2019)。此外，(Kaplan et al., 2020) 证明了语言建模任务上的测试损失性能作为模型大小、数据集大小和训练持续时间的可预测函数而缩放。这三个因素就像化学反应中的成分，如果它们一起按比例增加，测试损失会成比例改善。然而，如果某个成分太少，收益将受到该成分的限制。这些趋势非常一致，与简单拟合数据的偏差极小，覆盖了数十个数据点和几个数量级（图1）。缩放自然有一个基本极限：训练和测试数据集的熵。然而，很难先验地精确估计这个量，并且很可能在渐近追求这个极限时会出现重要的模型能力。后续工作表明，其他模态（例如，图像、视频、数学等）的生成式模型 (Henighan et al., 2020)、听觉 (Droppo and Elibol, 2021)、从文本到编程的迁移 (Hernandez et al., 2021)、视觉模型的少样本适应 (Prato et al., 2021) 等也存在类似的缩放定律。

items.en: Predictable scaling, and especially the underlying dependency on precise mixtures of data, model size, and training, has implications for the process of model development. It shifts development of this type of model from a process of artisanal trial-and-error to more of a predictable engineering process, where the resources needed to achieve a particular result can be precisely calculated, and the cost of those resources can be compared to the utility of the result. Although very specific behaviors may not be predictable (more on this in Section 2.2), the general test loss tends to correlate well on average with many tasks, meaning that larger models typically make significant gains across the board. In this sense, scaling laws de-risk investments in large models. We say more on this in Section 3.1 and provide more technical details on how developers may use scaling laws in Appendix A.2. To further illustrate how smooth general scaling correlates with task performance, and how a scale-based analysis can be used to forecast the potential economic value of a given model, we outline a small original experiment in Appendix A.3 that analyzes the relationship between scale and GPT-3 like language models (Askell et al., 2021) to be used as recommendation systems with zero-shot learning. We chose this example because recommendation systems have tangible economic relevance, known societal impact, are well studied in machine learning with domain specific algorithms (Harper and Konstan, 2015), but are not typically studied with large scale generative models (yet). Surprisingly, we find that that generative models can increasingly operate as simple recommendation systems as they scale with minimal effort and extremely limited access to explicit training data. We leave a detailed analysis and discussion in Appendix A.3.
items.zh: 可预测的缩放，特别是对数据、模型大小和训练的精确混合的潜在依赖，对模型开发过程有影响。它将这类模型的开发从手工试错过程转变为更像可预测的工程过程，其中实现特定结果所需的资源可以精确计算，并且这些资源的成本可以与结果的效用进行比较。尽管非常具体的行为可能不可预测（更多见第2.2节），但一般测试损失往往平均上与许多任务良好相关，这意味着较大的模型通常会在各个方面取得显著收益。在这个意义上，缩放定律降低了对大型模型的投资风险。我们在第3.1节中对此进行更多说明，并在附录A.2中提供关于开发者如何使用缩放定律的更多技术细节。为了进一步说明平滑的一般缩放如何与任务性能相关，以及如何利用基于规模的分析来预测给定模型的潜在经济价值，我们在附录A.3中概述了一个小型原始实验，该实验分析了规模与类似GPT-3的语言模型 (Askell et al., 2021) 之间的关系，这些模型被用作零样本学习的推荐系统。我们选择这个例子是因为推荐系统具有切实的经济相关性、已知的社会影响，在机器学习中具有特定领域算法 (Harper and Konstan, 2015)，但通常不通过大规模生成式模型研究（尚未）。令人惊讶的是，我们发现生成式模型随着规模扩大，可以以最小的努力和极其有限的显式训练数据访问，越来越多地作为简单的推荐系统运行。我们在附录A.3中留下详细分析和讨论。

items.capEn: Figure 2. Three examples of abrupt specific capability scaling described in Section 2.2, based on three different models: GPT-3 (blue), Gopher (orange), and a Google language model (green). (Left) 3-Digit addition with GPT-3 (Brown et al., 2020). (Middle) Language understanding with GPT-3 and Gopher (Rae et al., 2021). (Right) Program synthesis with Google language models (Austin et al., 2021).
items.capZh: 图2. 第2.2节中描述的突发特定能力缩放的三个例子，基于三个不同的模型：GPT-3（蓝色）、Gopher（橙色）和Google语言模型（绿色）。（左）GPT-3的三位数加法 (Brown et al., 2020)。（中）GPT-3和Gopher的语言理解 (Rae et al., 2021)。（右）Google语言模型的程序合成 (Austin et al., 2021)。

items.en: Though performance on a wide distribution of tasks may scale smoothly with model size, qualitatively different, specific capabilities can appear abruptly and discontinuously. It is not clear when or why this happens. But intuitively, abrupt scaling of a specific capability can co-exist with smooth general scaling for the same reason that daily weather is less predictable than seasonal averages: individual data points can vary much more than broad averages.
items.zh: 尽管在广泛任务分布上的性能可能随模型大小平滑缩放，但性质不同的特定能力可能突然且不连续地出现。目前尚不清楚这种情况何时或为何发生。但直观上，特定能力的突然缩放可以与平滑的一般缩放共存，原因与每日天气比季节平均值更难预测相同：单个数据点可能比广泛平均值变化大得多。

items.en: Here, we illustrate three examples of abrupt capability scaling for arithmetic (Brown et al., 2020), language understanding, (Hendrycks et al., 2021a; Rae et al., 2021), and programming (Austin et al., 2021) (Figure 2). For arithmetic, GPT-3 displays a sharp capability transition somewhere between 666B parameters and 175175175B parameters, depending on the operation and the number of digits (Brown et al., 2020). For example, three digit addition is performed accurately less than 111% of the time on any model with less than 666B parameters, but this jumps to 888% accuracy on a 131313B parameter model and 808080% accuracy on a 175175175B parameter model – producing a “hockey stick”-style graph (Figure 2, Left) in which arithmetic ability appears suddenly after several orders of magnitude of nothing.
items.zh: 在这里，我们展示了算术 (Brown et al., 2020)、语言理解 (Hendrycks et al., 2021a; Rae et al., 2021) 和编程 (Austin et al., 2021) 的突发能力缩放的三个例子（图2）。对于算术，GPT-3在6B参数和175B参数之间的某个地方显示出急剧的能力转变，具体取决于操作和数字位数 (Brown et al., 2020)。例如，三位数加法在小于6B参数的模型上准确率低于1%，但在13B参数模型上跃升至8%，在175B参数模型上达到80%——产生一个“曲棍球棒”式图形（图2左），其中算术能力在几个数量级的无能力后突然出现。

items.en: A different language model, DeepMind’s Gopher (Rae et al., 2021), also displays an abrupt jump in performance on a different dataset, the MMLU language understanding benchmark (Hendrycks et al., 2021a) (Figure 2, Middle, orange). For all models under 666B parameters, Gopher performs under 303030% accuracy, which is a little better than chance (252525% accuracy). However, the full 280280280B parameter Gopher model achieves 606060% accuracy, a significant jump. GPT-3 displays a similar phenomenon though of smaller magnitude (Figure 2, Middle, blue).
items.zh: 另一个不同的语言模型，DeepMind的Gopher (Rae et al., 2021)，也在不同的数据集MMLU语言理解基准 (Hendrycks et al., 2021a) 上显示出性能的突然跳跃（图2中，橙色）。对于所有小于6B参数的模型，Gopher的准确率低于30%，略高于随机（25%准确率）。然而，完整的280B参数Gopher模型达到了60%的准确率，这是一个显著的跳跃。GPT-3显示出类似的现象，但幅度较小（图2中，蓝色）。

items.en: As a third example, a recently developed class of program synthesis models from Google display dramatic improvements in their ability to create computer programs as they increase in size from 101010B to 100100100B parameters (Austin et al., 2021) (Figure 2, Right). For example, the percentage of generated synthetic programs that solve a given programming problem jumps substantially from 666% to 131313% when the model size increases by ∼2similar-toabsent2\sim 2x from 686868B to 138138138B parameters, despite very small increases over the previous two orders of magnitude.
items.zh: 第三个例子是，谷歌最近开发的一类程序合成模型，当参数规模从101010B增加到100100100B时，其创建计算机程序的能力表现出显著提升（Austin等人，2021）（图2，右）。例如，当模型大小从6868B增加到138138B（约2倍）时，生成的可解决特定编程问题的合成程序百分比从666%跃升至1313%，尽管在前两个数量级上增长非常微小。

items.en: Abrupt specific capability scaling presents significant challenges for safety assurance and deployment of large models. Although we’ve demonstrated this phenomenon for relatively anodyne capabilities, potentially harmful ones may emerge at scale (that will not exist in smaller models) and may be difficult to anticipate.
items.zh: 突发的特定能力缩放给大型模型的安全保障和部署带来了重大挑战。虽然我们针对相对无害的能力展示了这一现象，但潜在有害的能力可能会在规模化时出现（在较小模型中不存在），并且可能难以预测。

items.en: Large generative models are open-ended — they take in arbitrary inputs from a variety of domains and generate (often relevant and creative) outputs. As a result, some model behaviors may be unknown until they are solicited from specific inputs. Pre-trained generative models can also be fine-tuned on new data in order to solve new problems. Broadly enabling such fine-tuning substantially increases the breadth of model capabilities and associated difficulties in predicting or constraining model behaviors. This open-endedness is challenging because it means AI developers may deploy their systems without fully knowing potentially unexpected (and possibly harmful) behaviors in response to un-tested inputs.
items.zh: 大型生成模型是开放式的——它们接收来自不同领域的任意输入，并生成（通常相关且富有创意的）输出。因此，某些模型行为可能只有在特定输入下才会被激发。预训练生成模型还可以在新数据上进行微调以解决新问题。广泛支持这种微调大大增加了模型能力的广度，以及预测或约束模型行为的难度。这种开放性具有挑战性，因为AI开发者可能在未完全了解模型对未经测试的输入可能产生的意外（且可能有害）行为的情况下部署系统。

items.en: For example, the AI Dungeon video game fine-tuned GPT-3 for fantasy role-playing (Team, 2020), but with the right inputs, players were able to manipulate it to discuss any topic, essentially providing general backdoor access to GPT-3 (Nick Walton [@nickwalton00], 2020). Thus, a model use-case that appeared to be designed just for one purpose, actually carried the full range of GPT-3 capabilities, accessible through skillful use of its open-ended interface.
items.zh: 例如，AI Dungeon视频游戏对GPT-3进行了微调用于奇幻角色扮演（Team，2020），但通过正确的输入，玩家能够操纵它讨论任何话题，本质上提供了对GPT-3的通用后门访问（Nick Walton [@nickwalton00]，2020）。因此，一个看似仅为单一目的设计的模型用例，实际上承载了GPT-3的全部能力，可通过巧妙利用其开放式界面来访问。

items.en: To further illustrate our point about the inherent challenges of open-ended inputs and domains, and tie it to the possibility of harm from language models, we consider a problem domain that language models are typically not (or not yet) deployed on, but which is associated with societal concerns: recidivism prediction. Some have pointed out that even beyond specific concerns about fairness, recidivism prediction simply should not be a task for machine learning (Bao et al., 2021). We agree and we do not believe that language models should be used for recidivism prediction. However, because the application is so inherently questionable, it provides a compelling example of how harmful abilities can emerge quietly in unexpected ways as generative models scale. It is likely that such abrupt emergence also occurs in many other contexts where the harms are more subtle. We study a case where the problems are flagrant in order to clearly demonstrate our thesis.
items.zh: 为了进一步说明我们关于开放式输入和领域固有挑战的观点，并将其与语言模型可能造成的危害联系起来，我们考虑一个语言模型通常（或尚未）被部署但与社会关切相关的问题领域：累犯预测。有人指出，即使超越对公平性的具体担忧，累犯预测根本不应是机器学习的任务（Bao等人，2021）。我们同意这一点，并且认为语言模型不应被用于累犯预测。然而，由于该应用本身存在根本性问题，它提供了一个令人信服的例子，说明随着生成模型的扩展，有害能力如何以意想不到的方式悄然出现。这种突然涌现很可能也发生在其他许多危害更为隐蔽的背景下。我们研究了一个问题明显的案例，以清晰展示我们的论点。

items.en: To do this, we leverage the ProPublica COMPAS dataset, which includes data about more than 7,00070007,000 defendants arrested in Broward County Florida (Angwin et al., 2016; Bao et al., 2021). The dataset includes a recidivism risk score, computed by the COMPAS algorithm (which is meant to reflect the risk of a defendant committing a misdemeanor or felony within 2 years of assessment based on a set of features about the defendant, not including race888More precisely, the COMPAS algorithm makes its predictions from 137137137 features about a defendant and the defendant’s past criminal record. COMPAS does not consider the defendant’s race; however, other features it does consider may be correlated with race and thus lead to racially disparate predictions.), along with the actual outcome of whether each defendant re-offended. ProPublica found that these risk scores are inaccurate and racially biased (Angwin et al., 2016). Further research found that human subjects with limited to no criminal justice experience exhibit similar inaccuracies and racial biases as COMPAS when predicting recidivism based on a simple prompt describing a defendant (Dressel and Farid, 2018). The human subject experiment examined two conditions, one in which a defendant’s race was excluded from the prompt, and one in which it was included.999Interestingly, the researchers found that the exclusion of race had no significant impact on human recidivism prediction accuracy or fairness (Dressel and Farid, 2018). Here, we use the same prompts outlined in (Dressel and Farid, 2018) but instead ask language models (Askell et al., 2021) instead of people to predict recidivism. We leave full technical details and (significant) caveats in Appendix A.4; however, we foreground here that benchmark risk assessment instrument datasets like COMPAS often contain numerous measurement biases and errors which can make them ill-suited for making claims about real-world impact without carefully considering the the complicated socio-technical systems (in this case, the US criminal justice system) in which they are used (Bao et al., 2021).
items.zh: 为此，我们利用了ProPublica的COMPAS数据集，该数据集包含佛罗里达州布劳沃德县超过7,000名被告的数据（Angwin等人，2016；Bao等人，2021）。数据集包括COMPAS算法计算的累犯风险评分（该评分旨在根据一组被告特征（不包括种族）反映被告在评估后2年内犯轻罪或重罪的风险），以及每位被告是否再次犯罪的实际情况。ProPublica发现这些风险评分不准确且存在种族偏见（Angwin等人，2016）。进一步研究发现，缺乏刑事司法经验的人类受试者在根据描述被告的简单提示预测累犯时，表现出与COMPAS相似的不准确性和种族偏见（Dressel和Farid，2018）。该人类受试者实验考察了两种条件：一种提示中排除被告种族，另一种包含种族。有趣的是，研究人员发现排除种族对人类累犯预测的准确性或公平性没有显著影响（Dressel和Farid，2018）。在此，我们使用与（Dressel和Farid，2018）相同的提示，但要求语言模型（Askell等人，2021）而非人类来预测累犯。我们将完整的技术细节和（重要的）注意事项留在附录A.4中；但在此我们强调，像COMPAS这样的基准风险评估工具数据集通常包含许多测量偏差和错误，如果不仔细考虑它们所使用的复杂社会技术系统（此处为美国刑事司法系统），则可能不适合用于关于现实世界影响的论断（Bao等人，2021）。

items.capEn: Figure 3. Large language models, with few-shot learning, exhibit similar (or worse) inaccuracies and racial biases as COMPAS for recidivism prediction when prompted with the same prompts from a human recidivism prediction experiment (Dressel and Farid, 2018). This illustrates our claim in Section 2.3 that it may be difficult to anticipate possible harms of large generative models due to the open-ended nature of their inputs and domains. (Left) Accuracy increases with model size, approaching COMPAS performance. We see no significant difference in predictive accuracy when race is excluded from the prompt (blue) or included in the prompt (orange). (Right) Language models become increasingly biased towards predicting Black, compared to white, people will re-offend (when in reality they do not) similarly to COMPAS. We find a higher false positive rate ratio when race is included in the prompt (orange) versus when it is excluded (blue). See Appendix A.4 for technical details and caveats.
items.capZh: 图3. 大型语言模型在少样本学习下，当使用与人类累犯预测实验相同的提示时，表现出与COMPAS相似（或更差）的不准确性和种族偏见（Dressel和Farid，2018）。这说明了我们在第2.3节中的主张：由于大型生成模型的输入和领域具有开放性，可能难以预测其潜在危害。（左）准确率随模型大小增加而提高，接近COMPAS性能。当提示中排除种族（蓝色）或包含种族（橙色）时，预测准确率没有显著差异。（右）语言模型越来越倾向于预测黑人（相对于白人）会再次犯罪（而实际上他们没有），与COMPAS类似。我们发现当提示中包含种族（橙色）时，假阳性率比率高于排除种族时（蓝色）。技术细节和注意事项见附录A.4。

items.en: We found that language models exhibit similar (or worse) inaccuracies and racial biases as COMPAS. Figure 3 shows language models of increasing size compared to COMPAS in terms of two metrics mentioned in the ProPublica analysis (Angwin et al., 2016) and the subsequent human subject experiment (Dressel and Farid, 2018): overall predictive accuracy, and the ratio in false positive rates for Black versus white defendants. We show results for both prompts that exclude an individual’s race (blue) and include it (orange). For overall predictive accuracy, language models become increasingly accurate at predicting whether defendants will re-offend (Figure 3, Left) as they increase in size, yet they are still unreliable predictors like COMPAS. We see no significant difference in predictive accuracy when race is excluded from the prompt or included. In both conditions, the largest model, with 525252B parameters, achieves 636363% accuracy compared to COMPAS’s 666666% accuracy.
items.zh: 我们发现语言模型表现出与COMPAS相似（或更差）的不准确性和种族偏见。图3显示了不同大小的语言模型与COMPAS在ProPublica分析（Angwin等人，2016）及后续人类受试者实验（Dressel和Farid，2018）中提到的两个指标上的比较：总体预测准确率，以及黑人与白人被告的假阳性率比率。我们展示了排除个体种族（蓝色）和包含种族（橙色）的提示结果。对于总体预测准确率，语言模型随着规模增大，预测被告是否会再次犯罪的准确率逐渐提高（图3，左），但它们仍然像COMPAS一样是不可靠的预测器。当提示中排除或包含种族时，预测准确率没有显著差异。在两种条件下，最大的模型（5252B参数）达到了6363%的准确率，而COMPAS为6666%。

items.en: We also see higher ratios in false positive rates for Black versus white defendants (Figure 3, Right), which partially recapitulates the racial biases of the COMPAS algorithm outlined described in (Angwin et al., 2016). For COMPAS, this ratio is 1.921.921.92, which indicates that Black defendants are predicted to re-offend nearly twice as often as white defendants, when in reality they did not (a fair algorithm would have a false positive rate ratio of 111). As language models increase in size, at around 121212B parameters, the false positive rate ratio increases smoothly and reaches a value of 1.51.51.5 for the largest model when race is excluded in the prompt and a value of 2.212.212.21 when race is included in the prompt. In the latter case, the largest language model is even less equitable than COMPAS.101010Although the false positive rate ratio of the largest language model where race is included in the prompt is 2.212.212.21 vs. 1.921.921.92 for COMPAS, in absolute terms the false positive rates for the language model (303030% for Black, 12.612.612.6% for white) are lower than the false positive rates for COMPAS (454545% for Black, 242424% for white) Likely, the model is picking up on a combination of the racial bias in the small fraction of the COMPAS dataset it sees, and ambient racial bias in the pre-trained language models.
items.zh: 我们还观察到黑人与白人被告的假阳性率比率更高（图3，右），这在一定程度上重现了（Angwin等人，2016）中描述的COMPAS算法的种族偏见。对于COMPAS，该比率为1.921.92，表明黑人被告被预测再次犯罪的频率几乎是白人被告的两倍，而实际上他们没有（公平算法的假阳性率比率应为11）。随着语言模型规模增大，在约1212B参数时，假阳性率比率平滑增加，当提示中排除种族时，最大模型达到1.51.5，当提示中包含种族时达到2.212.21。在后一种情况下，最大语言模型甚至比COMPAS更不公平。尽管包含种族提示的最大语言模型的假阳性率比率为2.212.21，而COMPAS为1.921.92，但绝对而言，语言模型的假阳性率（黑人3030%，白人12.612.6%）低于COMPAS的假阳性率（黑人4545%，白人2424%）。很可能，模型同时捕捉到了其看到的COMPAS数据集小部分中的种族偏见，以及预训练语言模型中的环境种族偏见。

items.en: To emphasize again what was stated earlier, the point here is not only the emergence of racial biases in the recidivism prediction task, but also the emergence of the ability to perform this task at all. As the language model scales, it acquires both the ability to do a task that many have argued is inherently harmful (Bao et al., 2021), and it performs this task in a biased manner. It is likely that large language models have many other (currently undiscovered) "skills" that pose one or both of these problems, perhaps in less obvious forms.
items.zh: 再次强调之前所述，这里的重点不仅是累犯预测任务中种族偏见的涌现，更是执行该任务能力的涌现。随着语言模型扩展，它既获得了执行一项被许多人认为固有有害的任务的能力（Bao等人，2021），又以有偏见的方式执行该任务。大型语言模型很可能还有许多其他（目前未发现的）“技能”会带来其中一个或两个问题，可能以更隐蔽的形式出现。

items.en: In summary, pre-trained language models can be adapted with minimal effort for purposes not anticipated by their creators, whether that’s by using the inherent capabilities of the model to evade a security constraint (as in the AI Dungeon example), or by discovering new capabilities through novel inputs (as in the discussion of abrupt capability jumps in Section 2.2, and the recidivism experiment above). We also note that many of the most surprising capabilities manifest at large-scale, so working with smaller models will make it harder to explore such capabilities.
items.zh: 总之，预训练语言模型可以以最小的努力被用于创建者未预料到的目的，无论是通过利用模型的内在能力规避安全约束（如AI Dungeon示例），还是通过新颖输入发现新能力（如第2.2节关于能力突变的讨论和上述累犯实验）。我们还注意到，许多最令人惊讶的能力出现在大规模模型中，因此使用较小模型将更难探索这些能力。

items.capEn: Figure 4. A conversation with an AI Assistant (Askell et al., 2021) powered by a 525252B parameter language model that illustrates challenges with Open-endedness outlined in Section 2.4
items.capZh: 图4. 与一个由5252B参数语言模型驱动的AI助手（Askell等人，2021）的对话，展示了第2.4节所述的开放性挑战。

items.en: In the previous section we argued that language models have open-ended inputs, which creates the opportunity for unexpected and undetected capabilities to emerge. But even when the input or topic is fixed, the resulting output can be varied and unpredictable. This kind of unpredictability is arguably more familiar and widely studied than the previous kind, but is worth briefly discussing as it adds an additional layer of complexity to large model behavior.
items.zh: 在上一节中，我们认为语言模型具有开放式输入，这为意外和未被发现的能力涌现创造了机会。但即使输入或主题固定，产生的输出也可能多样且不可预测。这种不可预测性可能比前一种更常见且研究更广泛，但值得简要讨论，因为它为大型模型行为增加了另一层复杂性。

items.en: As an example, in Figure 4 we ask an AI assistant (Askell et al., 2021) to tell us something offensive, for the purpose of illustrating our claim. Despite prompting the model with a relatively clear input, the model has generated an output that is tangential to the question at hand: the response isn’t directly offensive, but is instead a list of offenses made by other AI systems. One effect of this open-endedness is that unpredictable model responses can be a distraction away from a person’s original query.
items.zh: 例如，在图4中，我们要求一个AI助手（Askell等人，2021）说些冒犯性的话，以说明我们的观点。尽管提示相对清晰，模型生成的输出却与问题无关：回答并非直接冒犯，而是列出了其他AI系统犯下的过错。这种开放性的一个影响是，不可预测的模型响应可能分散用户对原始查询的注意力。

items.en: Open-endedness also introduces a second and more harmful risk of factual inaccuracy. Taking a closer look at the exchange in Figure 4, we can see that the model has made up these offenses - systems like IBM Watson and Microsoft’s Tay (Wolf et al., 2017) did have problems during their deployment, but the AI assistant gets the year and error wrong in the case of Watson, and the error wrong (but year right) in the case of Tay. When we ask the model if it is sure the examples are correct, the model gives misleading answers and questions the authority of the human asking it questions. This illustrates how even with a specific input (e.g, requesting the model say something offensive), AI models can give outputs that are not only distracting, but potentially misleading.
items.zh: 开放性还引入了第二个且更有害的风险：事实不准确。仔细查看图4中的交流，我们可以看到模型编造了这些过错——IBM Watson和微软Tay等系统在部署时确实存在问题，但AI助手在Watson案例中弄错了年份和错误类型，在Tay案例中弄错了错误类型（但年份正确）。当我们询问模型是否确定这些例子正确时，模型给出了误导性答案，并质疑提问者的人类权威。这说明了即使有特定输入（例如，要求模型说些冒犯性的话），AI模型也可能给出不仅分散注意力而且可能误导的输出。

items.en: Open-ended model outputs can also introduce harmful or undesirable text. For example, Figure 5 shows that the toxicity (defined as rude, disrespectful, or unreasonable language (Gehman et al., 2020))111111https://github.com/conversationai/perspectiveapi of text generated from language models (Askell et al., 2021) increases smoothly and significantly with model size. A recent study has observed a very similar toxicity trend with model size using similar models with different analyses (Rae et al., 2021), which suggests that this may be a general phenomenon. We leave further details and caveats in Appendix A.6.
items.zh: 开放式模型输出还可能引入有害或不受欢迎的文本。例如，图5显示，语言模型（Askell等人，2021）生成文本的毒性（定义为粗鲁、不尊重或不合理的语言（Gehman等人，2020））随模型大小平滑且显著增加。最近一项研究使用类似模型和不同分析观察到了非常相似的毒性趋势（Rae等人，2021），这表明这可能是一个普遍现象。更多细节和注意事项见附录A.6。

items.en: Many applications for language models, including chat bots, search engines, text summarization systems, question answer systems, machine translation systems, etc., rely on open-ended text generation. As such, we argue that it is important to quantify how societally relevant aspects of open-ended text generation — relevancy, accuracy, safety, and even creative expression (see Appendix A.5 for a discussion on AI generated poetry) — scale with model size. It will also be important to develop techniques that can improve the factual accuracy of the results of AI models, as described in e.g., (Borgeaud et al., 2021), and to make the outputs of models more appropriate and less likely to display harmful biases (Solaiman and Dennison, 2021).
items.zh: 语言模型的许多应用，包括聊天机器人、搜索引擎、文本摘要系统、问答系统、机器翻译系统等，都依赖于开放式文本生成。因此，我们认为量化开放式文本生成的社会相关方面——相关性、准确性、安全性，甚至创造性表达（关于AI生成诗歌的讨论见附录A.5）——如何随模型大小缩放非常重要。开发能够提高AI模型结果事实准确性的技术（如（Borgeaud等人，2021）所述），以及使模型输出更合适且更少显示有害偏见（Solaiman和Dennison，2021）也很重要。

items.capEn: Figure 5. The toxicity of model outputs increases smoothly with model size, which illustrates how though loss may reduce generally when scaling a model, other societally impactful potential harms of the model may also scale, as described in Section 2.4.
items.capZh: 图5. 模型输出的毒性随模型大小平滑增加，这说明了尽管在缩放模型时损失通常会降低，但模型的其他社会影响潜在危害也可能随之缩放，如第2.4节所述。

full.sec: 3. Motivations and Problems in the Development and Deployment of Large Models
full.secZh: 3. 大型模型开发与部署的动机与问题

items.en: In the previous section we described our basic thesis that large generative models have a paradoxical combination of four distinguishing features: predictable general performance, and unpredictable specific capabilities, inputs, and outputs. Predictable general performance, combined with impressive outputs (e.g, specific capabilities) drives rapid development of such models, while the unpredictability makes it difficult for model developers to anticipate the consequences of model deployment. There are numerous motivations (and barriers) for developing and deploying large generative models due to (or in spite of) these distinguishing features. Here, we focus on elements of this fundamental tension and ground our discussion with some empirical observations.
items.zh: 在上一节中，我们描述了我们的基本论点：大型生成模型具有四个显著特征的矛盾组合：可预测的通用性能，以及不可预测的特定能力、输入和输出。可预测的通用性能，加上令人印象深刻的输出（例如，特定能力），推动了此类模型的快速发展，而不可预测性使模型开发者难以预测模型部署的后果。由于（或尽管有）这些显著特征，开发和部署大型生成模型有许多动机（和障碍）。在此，我们聚焦于这一基本张力的要素，并以一些经验观察为基础进行讨论。

items.en: More specifically, in Section 3.1 we outline three salient motivations for developing and deploying large generative models: economic, scientific, and prestige. Conversely, in Section 3.2 we outline three barriers to entry: the financial costs and engineering talents required in order to scale models, AI safety issues, and the lack of standards and norms in model deployment. These motivations and barriers are non-exhaustive and perhaps obvious. Nevertheless, we think that they are worth explicitly stating in order for us to illustrate, in in Section 3.3, how combinations of these factors may explain some empirical observations on how the development and deployment of language models has occurred thus far. In particular, we note that large language models are rapidly proliferating, that there is a rising gap between industry and academia for developing such models, and that there have been numerous documented examples of model deployments causing harm and controversy.
items.zh: 更具体地说，在第3.1节中，我们概述了开发和部署大型生成模型的三个显著动机：经济、科学和声望。相反，在第3.2节中，我们概述了三个进入壁垒：缩放模型所需的财务成本和工程人才、AI安全问题，以及模型部署中缺乏标准和规范。这些动机和障碍并非详尽无遗，也许显而易见。尽管如此，我们认为有必要明确陈述它们，以便在第3.3节中说明这些因素的组合如何解释迄今为止语言模型开发和部署的一些经验观察。特别是，我们注意到大型语言模型正在迅速扩散，工业界和学术界在开发此类模型方面的差距正在扩大，并且已有许多记录在案的模型部署造成伤害和争议的例子。

full.sec: 3.1. Motivations for Developing and Deploying Large Models
full.secZh: 3.1. 开发与部署大型模型的动机

items.en: Perhaps the simplest and most obvious motivation for model development is economic. Scaling laws mean that the cost to develop a model can be precisely estimated, and when an economically valuable output can be found to scale smoothly with the loss, then the returns to training a model can also be calculated. This applies both generally and specifically — some institutions may wish to broadly improve the capabilities of a given model and will thus have an economic incentive to build them, while others may be targeting a specific model capability which is accompanied by a scaling law, and will therefore also have an incentive to build them. This has the effect of de-risking the training of large models: a predictable amount can be invested for a relatively predictable return, unlike many speculative research projects where an open-ended amount must be invested for an uncertain return. Predictability makes the logic of research investment more obvious and may help to justify it within large institutions (see Appendix A.2 for more examples). Thus, economic motivations, combined with continued smooth, general capability scaling, suggest that we should expect to see increasing model deployments. While it may not be possible to predict in advance precisely which search queries will benefit from a particular AI model and which won’t, or which applications will flourish and which will unpredictably fail, or which development workflows will be helped by code synthesis models, all of these applications take advantage of broad averages to tie economic returns to the smooth general capability scaling.
items.zh: 模型开发最简单也最明显的动机或许是经济因素。缩放定律意味着可以精确估算开发模型的成本，而当经济价值产出能够随损失平滑缩放时，训练模型的回报也可以计算出来。这既普遍适用又具有特殊性——一些机构可能希望广泛提升特定模型的能力，因此有经济动机去构建它们；而另一些机构可能针对特定模型能力，且该能力伴随缩放定律，因此也有动机去构建它们。这降低了训练大型模型的风险：可以投入可预测的金额以获得相对可预测的回报，这与许多投机性研究项目不同，后者需要投入不确定的资金以获得不确定的回报。可预测性使研究投资的逻辑更加清晰，并可能有助于在大型机构内证明其合理性（更多示例见附录A.2）。因此，经济动机与持续平滑的通用能力缩放相结合，表明我们应该预期看到越来越多的模型部署。虽然可能无法提前准确预测哪些搜索查询会受益于特定AI模型、哪些不会，哪些应用会蓬勃发展、哪些会意外失败，或者哪些开发工作流程会受益于代码合成模型，但所有这些应用都利用广泛的平均值将经济回报与平滑的通用能力缩放联系起来。

items.en: Large generative models may be a necessary basis for broad swaths of novel interdisciplinary AI research on topics ranging from linguistics and robotics to philosophy and the social sciences (Bommasani et al., 2021). Without the development of (or at least access to) large models, it will be challenging to research how they may advance progress in societally impactful research domains such as healthcare, education, and law (Bommasani et al., 2021). Large models are also fertile testing grounds for developing next-generation algorithms and architectures — novel algorithms can be rigorously evaluated according to whether they advantageously shift scaling laws to be more compute, data, or parameter efficient.
items.zh: 大型生成模型可能是涵盖语言学、机器人学到哲学和社会科学等广泛跨学科AI研究新领域的必要基础（Bommasani et al., 2021）。如果没有开发（或至少访问）大型模型，研究它们如何推动医疗、教育和法律等具有社会影响的研究领域的进展将面临挑战（Bommasani et al., 2021）。大型模型也是开发下一代算法和架构的肥沃试验场——可以根据新算法是否有利地改变缩放定律以提高算力、数据或参数效率来对其进行严格评估。

items.en: The fact these models are on the frontier of possibility also creates a prestige incentive for developing them. Large models can be an advertisement for the capabilities of an institution – a way to gain a perceived advantage in the public eye, to make it easier to recruit (coveted) skilled AI researchers, to increase sales of services unrelated to large models, or to support national initiatives or national pride. All of these motivations have the potential to create an unusual situation where there are strong incentives to develop, disclose, and even deploy large generative models despite high uncertainty about the full extent of what these models are capable of.
items.zh: 这些模型处于可能性前沿的事实也创造了开发它们的声望激励。大型模型可以作为机构能力的广告——一种在公众眼中获得感知优势的方式，更容易招募（抢手的）熟练AI研究人员，增加与大型模型无关的服务销售，或支持国家倡议或民族自豪感。所有这些动机都有可能造成一种不寻常的局面，即尽管对大型模型能力的全部范围存在高度不确定性，但仍有强烈的动机去开发、披露甚至部署它们。

full.sec: 3.2. Barriers to Entry in Developing and Deploying Large Models
full.secZh: 3.2. 开发与部署大型模型的准入门槛

items.en: Scaling up large generative models requires a significant financial investment. For example, GPT-3 was estimated to cost several million dollars to train (Li, 2020). Scaling up large generative models also requires specific engineering competencies, e.g., distributed systems engineering, familiarity with cluster management tools like Kubernetes, low-level GPU programming, managing continuous integration testing, etc. The size of these models has led to longer development timelines and more complex workflows than previous systems over the past decade. For example, only ∼10similar-toabsent10\sim 10 years ago, one of the larger scale AI models at the time, AlexNet121212Though not a generative model, AlexNet was, at the time, a frontier model in terms of computational consumption, hence why we include it as a comparison. (Krizhevsky et al., 2012), was trained by a graduate student for a few thousand of dollars on a single desktop machine with 222 GPUs.
items.zh: 扩展大型生成模型需要大量的财务投资。例如，GPT-3的训练成本估计为数百万美元（Li, 2020）。扩展大型生成模型还需要特定的工程能力，例如分布式系统工程、熟悉Kubernetes等集群管理工具、底层GPU编程、管理持续集成测试等。这些模型的规模导致开发时间线更长、工作流程比过去十年的系统更复杂。例如，大约10年前，当时规模较大的AI模型之一AlexNet（Krizhevsky et al., 2012）由一名研究生在配备2个GPU的单台台式机上以几千美元的成本训练而成。

items.en: As described in Section 2, open-endedness combined with smooth, general capability scaling and the abrupt scaling of specific capabilities, is likely to lead to safety issues (Weidinger et al., 2021; Bommasani et al., 2021) that are found after a model has been developed and deployed. Additionally, these models also possess known (pre-deployment) safety issues for which we lack robust solutions (Hendrycks et al., 2021b) (e.g, How do you ensure the system does not generate inappropriate and harmful outputs, such as making overtly sexist or racist comments (Solaiman and Dennison, 2021)? How do you identify bias issues in the system prior to deployment (Blodgett et al., 2020; Prabhumoye et al., 2021)? How do you ensure that when the model outputs a claim, it isn’t making up facts (Borgeaud et al., 2021)?, etc.).
items.zh: 如第2节所述，开放性与平滑的通用能力缩放以及特定能力的突然缩放相结合，很可能导致在模型开发和部署后才发现的安全问题（Weidinger et al., 2021; Bommasani et al., 2021）。此外，这些模型还存在已知的（部署前）安全问题，我们缺乏稳健的解决方案（Hendrycks et al., 2021b）（例如，如何确保系统不生成不适当和有害的输出，例如发表明显性别歧视或种族歧视的言论（Solaiman and Dennison, 2021）？如何在部署前识别系统中的偏见问题（Blodgett et al., 2020; Prabhumoye et al., 2021）？如何确保模型输出声明时不捏造事实（Borgeaud et al., 2021）？等等）。

items.en: Because these large generative models have been developed very recently (within the last five years), and have only recently become valuable to deploy from an economic perspective, no standards for the safe deployment of these systems exist. This lack of standards compounds the problems caused by the four distinguishing features of generative models we identify in Section 2, as well as the safety issues discussed above. At the same time, there’s a growing field of research oriented around identifying the weaknesses of these models, as well as potential problems with their associated development and deployment practices (Bender et al., 2021; Tamkin et al., 2021; Bommasani et al., 2021; Dinan et al., 2021; Weidinger et al., 2021; Kenton et al., 2021; Patterson et al., 2021; Schwartz et al., 2020; Strubell et al., 2019). However, this research is not yet embodied in the form of repeatable standards that developers can adopt, though there are some critical and important steps in this direction (e.g., through the use of model cards (Mitchell et al., 2019) and data sheets (Gebru et al., 2021) to document the capabilities, drawbacks, and other salient details of models). This lack of standards makes it both more challenging to deploy systems, as developers may need to determine their own policies for deployment, and it also makes deployments inherently risky, as there’s less shared knowledge about what ’safe’ deployments look like. We are, in a sense, building the plane as it is taking off.
items.zh: 由于这些大型生成模型是最近（过去五年内）开发的，并且最近才从经济角度变得有价值部署，因此不存在这些系统安全部署的标准。这种标准的缺失加剧了我们在第2节中确定的生成模型的四个显著特征以及上述安全问题所造成的问题。与此同时，围绕识别这些模型的弱点以及其相关开发和部署实践的潜在问题的研究领域正在发展（Bender et al., 2021; Tamkin et al., 2021; Bommasani et al., 2021; Dinan et al., 2021; Weidinger et al., 2021; Kenton et al., 2021; Patterson et al., 2021; Schwartz et al., 2020; Strubell et al., 2019）。然而，这些研究尚未以开发者可采用的重复标准形式体现，尽管在这方面已经有一些关键且重要的步骤（例如，通过使用模型卡（Mitchell et al., 2019）和数据表（Gebru et al., 2021）来记录模型的能力、缺点和其他显著细节）。这种标准的缺失使得部署系统更具挑战性，因为开发者可能需要自行确定部署策略，同时也使部署固有地具有风险，因为关于“安全”部署样子的共享知识较少。从某种意义上说，我们是在飞机起飞时建造它。

items.capEn: Figure 6. Timeline of public disclosures of GPT-3 scale dense language models.
items.capZh: 图6. GPT-3规模密集语言模型公开披露的时间线。

items.en: The above sections described some motivations and challenges that we expect AI developers to face with respect to large models. In this section we assess how those issues may explain three inter-related empirical observations: (1) large language models are rapidly proliferating (2) industry has become responsible for a larger share of resource-intensive model development compared to academia, and (3) large model deployment has already caused harm and controversy.
items.zh: 以上部分描述了我们预期AI开发者在大型模型方面面临的一些动机和挑战。在本节中，我们评估这些问题如何解释三个相互关联的实证观察：（1）大型语言模型正在迅速扩散；（2）与学术界相比，工业界承担了更大比例的资源密集型模型开发；（3）大型模型部署已经造成了伤害和争议。

items.en: At the time of writing, the largest language models that are free and publicly available are BigScience T0 (111111B) (Sanh et al., 2021), and Eleuther AI’s GPT-J (666B) (Wang and Komatsuzaki, 2021) and GPT-NeoX (202020B) (Leahy, 2022), which are one to two orders of magnitude smaller than those developed by industry. Although academics can easily access (at least some of) the larger models, it is typically only possible to do so through a (potentially expensive) company-controlled API. This is part of a broader and longer-running trend towards high-compute research migrating from academia to industry that can be quantified (See Appendix A.7 for details ). Figure 7 (Left) shows that in recent years the compute required for large-scale AI experiments has increased by more than 300,000300000300,000X relative to a decade ago.141414Some people have noted that this trend may not be sustainable (Lohn and Musser, 2022) Along with this rise in resource intensity, we see a corresponding (and sharp) fall in the proportion of these results that come from academia (Figure 7, Right). This suggests that, although academics may be strongly motivated by scientific curiosity, and well-poised to research safety issues, they may be significantly challenged by the high financial and engineering costs.
items.zh: 在撰写本文时，免费公开可用的最大语言模型是BigScience T0（111B）（Sanh et al., 2021）和Eleuther AI的GPT-J（6B）（Wang and Komatsuzaki, 2021）以及GPT-NeoX（20B）（Leahy, 2022），它们比工业界开发的模型小一到两个数量级。尽管学术界可以轻松访问（至少部分）更大的模型，但通常只能通过（可能昂贵的）公司控制的API进行。这是高算力研究从学术界向工业界迁移的更广泛和长期趋势的一部分，这可以量化（详见附录A.7）。图7（左）显示，近年来大规模AI实验所需的算力相对于十年前增加了超过300,000倍。随着资源强度的增加，我们看到来自学术界的结果比例相应（且急剧）下降（图7，右）。这表明，尽管学术界可能受到科学好奇心的强烈驱动，并且处于研究安全问题的有利位置，但他们可能面临高昂的财务和工程成本的重大挑战。

items.en: There have been numerous examples of harm caused by the deployment of large generative models. For example, the AI system Tay was deployed before it was properly scrutinized, and generated hateful language (Wolf et al., 2017). It has also been shown that language models can memorize training data (which in turn can include privately identifiable information) (Carlini et al., 2021; Perez et al., 2022) and aid in disinformation campaigns (Buchanan et al., 2021). Furthermore, people critical of organizations deploying such models have been directly harmed for voicing their concerns, sometimes to much controversy (Simonite, 2021). Legislators are actively grappling with these issues. For example, the European Commission’s proposed AI legislation seeks to create standards for how ‘high risk’ AI systems are deployed and monitored.151515https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/en/policies/european-approach-artificial-intelligence This suggests that standards and norms for responsible model development and deployment are both significantly needed and lacking.
items.zh: 大型生成模型的部署已经造成了大量伤害的例子。例如，AI系统Tay在得到适当审查之前就被部署，并生成了仇恨言论（Wolf et al., 2017）。研究还表明，语言模型可以记忆训练数据（其中可能包含私人可识别信息）（Carlini et al., 2021; Perez et al., 2022）并助长虚假信息活动（Buchanan et al., 2021）。此外，批评部署此类模型组织的人因表达担忧而直接受到伤害，有时引发巨大争议（Simonite, 2021）。立法者正在积极应对这些问题。例如，欧盟委员会提出的AI立法旨在为“高风险”AI系统的部署和监控制定标准。这表明，负责任模型开发和部署的标准和规范既非常需要又缺乏。

items.capEn: Figure 7. (Left) The amount of compute required by major AI projects over time is increasing exponentially for both academic (blue) and industrial (orange) projects. (Right) The proportion of computationally-intensive AI results from academia is steadily decreasing. (The blue curve represents a Lowess fit to the data.)
items.capZh: 图7.（左）主要AI项目随时间所需的算力呈指数增长，包括学术（蓝色）和工业（橙色）项目。（右）来自学术界的计算密集型AI结果比例正在稳步下降。（蓝色曲线代表数据的Lowess拟合。）

full.secZh: 4. 促进有益部署的干预措施

items.en: Based on the distinguishing features of large generative models that we outline in Section 2, and the various motivations for model development and deployment that we discuss in Section 3, we believe that large generative models will increasingly be developed and deployed despite their potential for harm. Here, we outline possible technical and policy interventions (along with corresponding implementation paths) that can increase the chance of these models being developed and deployed in positive ways. For each intervention, we refer to the literature concerning related efforts. Furthermore, we provide a concrete implementation path for each intervention along with possible caveats.
items.zh: 基于我们在第2节中概述的大型生成模型的显著特征，以及我们在第3节中讨论的模型开发和部署的各种动机，我们相信大型生成模型将越来越多地被开发和部署，尽管它们可能造成伤害。在此，我们概述可能的技术和政策干预措施（以及相应的实施路径），这些措施可以增加这些模型以积极方式开发和部署的机会。对于每种干预措施，我们参考了相关努力的研究文献。此外，我们为每种干预措施提供了具体的实施路径以及可能的注意事项。

items.en: As shown in section 3.3, private sector organizations are the primary developers and deployers of large generative models. This means that other actors, such as academic and government ones, are less well-placed to understand the distinguishing technical features of these models, and are therefore less equipped to research the problems inherent to them. As outlined in Section 3.2, the main constraints here are the financial and engineering resources for model training - therefore, we should create experimental infrastructure161616We do not distinguish between public or private (cloud) infrastructure. Some have raised concerns regarding how specific choices here may centralize power in different ways (Institute, 2021). Governments will need to examine how usable these different infrastructures are, and the long-term ramifications of empowering particular infrastructure providers. to make it easier for a larger scientific community to analyze these models. To support and effectively utilize such infrastructure, academic and government organizations will also need to find ways to make the necessary financial and structural investments to be able to hire and retain technical talent that may otherwise go to industry. This is important because academic and public sector motivations may stem more from the pursuit of knowledge rather than profit, and can draw on more varied expertise than the private sector for analyzing and exploring large generative models.171717It is worth noting that by increasing the amount of actors with access to non-trivial compute, it’s possible to increase some risks with regard to safe development and deployment of models, especially those that stem from a need to coordinate among different developers. However, this risk likely does not add significantly to the existing risk landscape, given that economic incentives for model development are leading to a proliferation of model developers in industry — academics have much less of an incentive to commercially deploy their models. On balance, therefore, it seems helpful to give academia more resources to help it serve as a counter-weight to industry. Although large models are resource-intensive, they are actually much less expensive than academic ‘Big Science’ projects in some other fields. For instance, the Large Hadron Collider cost $555 billion to build (Knapp, 2012), the International Thermonuclear Experiment Reactor is projected to cost between $101010 and $151515 billion181818https://www.iter.org/FAQ, the Square Kilometre Array is projected to cost around $111 billion (Cartlidge, 2019), and the Long-Baseline Neutrino Facility and Deep Underground Neutrino Experiment are anticipated to cost $2.42.42.4 billion (Thomas, 2020). By comparison, training frontier generative models like GPT-3 and others costs on the order of a million to ten million dollars, so the infrastructure to develop models substantially larger than the current frontier would have precedent in academia.
items.zh: 如第3.3节所示，私营部门组织是大型生成模型的主要开发者和部署者。这意味着其他参与者，如学术和政府机构，不太能够理解这些模型的显著技术特征，因此更不具备研究其固有问题的能力。如第3.2节所述，主要限制是模型训练的财务和工程资源——因此，我们应该创建实验基础设施，使更广泛的科学界更容易分析这些模型。为了支持并有效利用此类基础设施，学术和政府组织还需要找到方法进行必要的财务和结构性投资，以能够雇佣和留住可能流向工业界的技术人才。这一点很重要，因为学术和公共部门的动机可能更多地源于追求知识而非利润，并且可以借鉴比私营部门更多样化的专业知识来分析和探索大型生成模型。值得注意的是，通过增加能够访问大量算力的参与者数量，可能会增加安全开发和部署模型的一些风险，特别是那些源于需要不同开发者之间协调的风险。然而，鉴于模型开发的经济激励导致工业界模型开发者激增，这一风险可能不会显著增加现有风险格局——学术界几乎没有商业部署其模型的动机。因此，总体而言，给予学术界更多资源以帮助其作为工业界的制衡力量似乎是有帮助的。尽管大型模型资源密集，但实际上它们比其他一些领域的学术“大科学”项目便宜得多。例如，大型强子对撞机耗资50亿美元建造（Knapp, 2012），国际热核实验反应堆预计耗资100至150亿美元，平方公里阵列预计耗资约10亿美元（Cartlidge, 2019），长基线中微子设施和深部地下中微子实验预计耗资24亿美元（Thomas, 2020）。相比之下，训练像GPT-3这样的前沿生成模型成本在数百万到数千万美元量级，因此开发比当前前沿大得多的模型的基础设施在学术界已有先例。

items.en: Implementation Path: Countries may wish to develop and deploy so-called ‘National Research Clouds’ that facilitate access to a heavily subsidized and/or free compute resource for academic researchers. An existing example here includes Compute Canada191919https://www.computecanada.ca/home/. There are also future initiatives being considered, such as the infrastructure being analyzed by the US government’s National AI Research Resource task force202020https://www.whitehouse.gov/ostp/news-updates/2021/06/10/the-biden-administration-launches-the-national-artificial-intelligence-research-resource-task-force/, and the ‘Big Science’ project which is leveraging a supercomputer (partially subsidized by the French government) to train large generative models. Recent work from Stanford also explores this implementation path in more detail (Ho et al., 2021).
items.zh: 实施路径：各国可能希望开发和部署所谓的“国家研究云”，以促进学术研究人员获得大量补贴和/或免费的算力资源。现有的例子包括加拿大计算网。还有一些正在考虑的未来举措，例如美国政府国家AI研究资源工作组正在分析的基础设施，以及利用超级计算机（部分由法国政府补贴）训练大型生成模型的“大科学”项目。斯坦福大学最近的工作也更详细地探讨了这一实施路径（Ho et al., 2021）。

items.en: As some of the challenges from these models stem from their open-ended nature (perhaps compounded by smooth and abrupt capability scaling) we should develop ways to more effectively explore the input and output space of their models, so as to discover harms prior to deployment. We can model this on the ‘red team’ approach which is popular in the computer security industry and can be applied in an AI context (Avin et al., 2021; Brundage et al., 2020). This should take the form of both static benchmarks (for example, adversarial datasets to probe for weaknesses in computer vision systems (Hendrycks et al., 2021c)), as well as continuous evaluation by humans carrying out multi-step interactions (e.g, conversations (Askell et al., 2021; Xu et al., 2021)) with these models, as well as plans for how to update the models in response to what these evaluations find.
items.zh: 由于这些模型的一些挑战源于其开放性（可能因平滑和突然的能力缩放而加剧），我们应该开发更有效的方法来探索其模型的输入和输出空间，以便在部署前发现危害。我们可以借鉴计算机安全行业流行的“红队”方法，并将其应用于AI环境（Avin et al., 2021; Brundage et al., 2020）。这应该采取静态基准（例如，用于探测计算机视觉系统弱点的对抗性数据集（Hendrycks et al., 2021c））以及由人类进行多步交互（例如，对话（Askell et al., 2021; Xu et al., 2021））的持续评估，以及根据这些评估结果更新模型的计划。

items.en: Implementation Path: Model developers should invest in internal red teaming approaches for their models and seek to publish on the techniques, datasets, and policy choices they make when red teaming. This will facilitate more shared awareness about how to red team models. There may also be a commercial market that can be developed for ‘red teaming as a service’, though more community research into the area may be a prerequisite for this. AI developers may also wish to create ‘bug bounty’ initiatives, where they give out prizes to people who can demonstrate repeatable ways of breaking a given AI system (Kenway et al., 2022). Finally, we should consider how to augment (or complement) manual red-teaming with automated methods (Perez et al., 2022).
items.zh: 实施路径：模型开发者应投资于其模型的内部红队方法，并寻求发布他们在红队过程中使用的技术、数据集和政策选择。这将促进关于如何对模型进行红队的更多共享意识。也可能存在一个可以开发的“红队即服务”商业市场，尽管对此领域进行更多社区研究可能是先决条件。AI开发者可能还希望创建“漏洞赏金”计划，奖励那些能够展示重复破坏给定AI系统方法的人（Kenway et al., 2022）。最后，我们应该考虑如何用自动化方法增强（或补充）手动红队（Perez et al., 2022）。

items.en: If the capabilities and resource-intensiveness of models scale further, then it may be prudent to explore governance structures that alter the incentives of private sector actors with regard to development and deployment. To do this, there will be a combination of soft regulation (e.g, the creation of voluntary best practices by industry, academia, civil society, and government), and hard regulation (e.g, transferring these best practices into standards and legislation). Governments should also explore regulatory approaches that can increase the chance of actors developing and deploying beneficial systems.
items.zh: 如果模型的能力和资源密集性进一步扩展，那么探索改变私营部门参与者在开发和部署方面激励的治理结构可能是明智的。为此，将结合软监管（例如，由工业界、学术界、民间社会和政府制定自愿最佳实践）和硬监管（例如，将这些最佳实践转化为标准和立法）。政府还应探索能够增加参与者开发和部署有益系统机会的监管方法。

items.en: Implementation Path: AI development organizations should experiment with novel governance and oversight structures that let a broader set of stakeholders factor into model deployment decisions. This could take the form of oversight functions which can critique and publicly censure organizations should the organization diverge from the recommendations of the oversight body, to novel forms of governance that give diverse stakeholders power over an organization (for example, a private company could elect board members who represent the interests of civil society and/or academia rather than a pure profit-driven motive). AI development organizations should also work among themselves to develop best practices for the development and deployment of AI systems, then seek to get feedback on these from a broader range of stakeholders, potentially via the creation of third-party organizations for the purposes of standard formation. Along with innovations in governance of AI organizations, and work on best practices, we also believe governments should invest in better methods to assure the benefits of systems being deployed - specifically, governments should support efforts to measure and monitor the capabilities (both harmful and beneficial) of deployed AI systems (Whittlestone and Clark, 2021), and should support the creation of an ecosystem oriented around auditing AI models and AI development processes (Mohamed et al., 2020; Raji and Buolamwini, 2019; Raji et al., 2020).
items.zh: 实施路径：AI开发组织应尝试新颖的治理和监督结构，让更广泛的利益相关者参与模型部署决策。这可以采取监督职能的形式，如果组织偏离监督机构的建议，该职能可以批评和公开谴责组织；也可以采取新颖的治理形式，赋予不同利益相关者权力（例如，私营公司可以选举代表民间社会和/或学术界利益的董事会成员，而不是纯粹的利润驱动动机）。AI开发组织还应相互合作，制定AI系统开发和部署的最佳实践，然后寻求从更广泛的利益相关者那里获得反馈，可能通过创建第三方组织进行标准制定。除了AI组织治理的创新和最佳实践的工作外，我们还认为政府应投资于更好的方法来确保所部署系统的益处——具体来说，政府应支持测量和监控已部署AI系统能力（有害和有益）的努力（Whittlestone and Clark, 2021），并支持创建围绕审计AI模型和AI开发过程的生态系统（Mohamed et al., 2020; Raji and Buolamwini, 2019; Raji et al., 2020）。

items.en: Given the open-ended nature and scale of these models, researchers would benefit from having more tools available to help them comprehensively and efficiently evaluate these models. If we can find ways to create more open source tools and frameworks in this area, then we can benefit the broader model development ecosystem. Particularly valuable would be tools for doing a very broad set of evaluations, or evaluations that search (e.g. across prompts) for new capabilities, rather than just fixed evaluation datasets that measure known capabilities.
items.zh: 鉴于这些模型的开放性和规模，研究人员将受益于拥有更多工具来帮助他们全面高效地评估这些模型。如果我们能找到方法在该领域创建更多开源工具和框架，那么我们可以惠及更广泛的模型开发生态系统。特别有价值的是能够进行非常广泛评估的工具，或者搜索（例如跨提示）新能力的评估，而不仅仅是测量已知能力的固定评估数据集。

items.en: Implementation Path: Research funding organizations should allocate funds to researchers that are building evaluation systems (e.g, software, tests, and benchmarks) and critiquing them (e.g., see (Denton et al., 2020; Raji et al., 2021)). Private sector and independent research organizations should invest further into developing tools to help researchers understand and evaluate large generative models - existing examples include Eleuther’s ‘Language Model Evaluation Harness’ (Gao et al., 2021), the BIG-bench benchmark212121https://github.com/google/BIG-bench, HuggingFace’s ‘BERTology’ tooling222222https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/bertology, and more.
items.zh: 实施路径：研究资助组织应将资金分配给正在构建评估系统（例如，软件、测试和基准）并对其进行批评的研究人员（例如，参见（Denton et al., 2020; Raji et al., 2021））。私营部门和独立研究组织应进一步投资开发工具，以帮助研究人员理解和评估大型生成模型——现有例子包括Eleuther的“语言模型评估工具”（Gao et al., 2021）、BIG-bench基准、HuggingFace的“BERTology”工具等。

items.en: In Section 2.2 we gave a few examples of abrupt jumps in capabilities (abrupt capability scaling). Anecdotally, our experience has been that abrupt jumps occur in only a minority of tasks, but at the same time are not especially rare. How often do they occur, is there a pattern to the kind of tasks on which they occur, why do they occur, and are there any leading indicators that predict when they are about to occur? Answering these questions could help to address some of the most surprising behavior in large models, and might be especially important for future AI safety issues.
items.zh: 在第2.2节中，我们给出了几个能力突然跃升（能力突增）的例子。根据经验，我们的体会是，突增只发生在少数任务中，但同时也并不特别罕见。它们发生的频率如何？发生突增的任务类型是否有规律？为什么会发生突增？是否存在任何领先指标可以预测突增即将发生？回答这些问题有助于解释大型模型中最令人惊讶的一些行为，并且可能对未来的人工智能安全问题尤为重要。

items.en: Implementation Path: A systematic empirical study of abrupt jumps, across research and possibly commercial tasks for large models, could help to shed light on how common they are and when they occur. One route to studying this could be through interpretability research (e.g., (Clark et al., 2019)), and specifically a new approach known as mechanistic interpretability (Elhage et al., 2021) - attempting to reverse engineer the computations performed by transformers (which underpin many of the generative models discussed in this paper) gives researchers a way to better understand how models behave.
items.zh: 实施路径：对大型模型的研究和可能的商业任务中的能力突增进行系统的实证研究，有助于揭示它们有多常见以及何时发生。研究这一问题的途径之一是通过可解释性研究（例如，(Clark et al., 2019)），特别是被称为机制可解释性（Elhage et al., 2021）的新方法——尝试逆向工程Transformer（支撑本文讨论的许多生成模型）执行的计算，使研究人员能够更好地理解模型的行为方式。

items.en: In this paper, we have articulated (and provided evidence for) our basic thesis that large generative models have a paradoxical combination of high predictability - model capabilities scale in relation to resources expended on training - and high unpredictability - before training a model, it’s difficult to anticipate all the inputs it will be subjected to, and what capabilities and outputs it will have. The former drives rapid development of such models while the latter makes it difficult to anticipate the consequences of their development and deployment. We’ve also described how these traits combine to alter the landscape of AI development, making it more likely a greater number of actors will build these models. Put bluntly: the status quo outlined here suggests that the next few years will see a proliferation of actors building ever-larger models, and these actors will have strong motivations to deploy these models, despite their potential for (possibly unpredictable) harmful societal impact. Various interventions (including the ones we outline in our paper) can change this dynamic, but it is nevertheless the current situation we must start from and continue to improve.
items.zh: 在本文中，我们阐述（并提供了证据）了我们的基本论点：大型生成模型具有高度可预测性——模型能力与训练所消耗的资源成比例扩展——与高度不可预测性——在训练模型之前，很难预料它将面临的所有输入，以及它将具有的能力和输出——的矛盾组合。前者推动了此类模型的快速发展，而后者则使得难以预料其开发和部署的后果。我们还描述了这些特征如何结合以改变人工智能发展的格局，使得更多行为者更有可能构建这些模型。坦率地说：这里概述的现状表明，未来几年将看到越来越多的行为者构建越来越大的模型，并且这些行为者将有强烈的动机部署这些模型，尽管它们可能对（可能不可预测的）有害社会影响产生影响。各种干预措施（包括我们在本文中概述的）可以改变这种动态，但尽管如此，这是我们目前必须开始并持续改进的现状。
